HOME.page.title.message: OOMOL - A CLI that turns apps into tools for agents

HOME.page.description.message: Let Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code, and other agents use your apps, APIs, and internal tools. When ready-made tools stop short, keep composing and extending with OOMOL Studio.

HOME.FirstScreen.slogan.message: Build and Deliver Capabilities for Agents

HOME.FirstScreen.kicker.message: A capability production and delivery path for developers

HOME.FirstScreen.script.message: Start with ready-made tools in oo-cli. When they stop short, use OOMOL Studio and Cloud to compose and extend your own tools.

HOME.FirstScreen.heroTitle.message:
One CLI lets agents
use the apps you already use

HOME.FirstScreen.heroSubtitle.message: Start with ready-made tools in CLI. When they stop short, keep composing and extending with OOMOL Studio.

HOME.FirstScreen.heroLead.message: Let Codex, OpenClaw, Claude Code, and other agents use your apps, APIs, and internal tools. Then use OOMOL Studio to compose and extend your own tools.

HOME.FirstScreen.offer1.message: Use your own AI model. GLM-5 recommended.

HOME.FirstScreen.offer2.message: 200 free Cloud Task minutes each month

HOME.PainPoints.title.message: Once one tool is connected, agents can do things like this

HOME.PainPoints.subtitle.message: Start with outcomes, then explain how it connects.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.title.message: Send a PR summary to Slack

HOME.PainPoints.card1.description.message: Turn code changes and team communication into one action.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.prompt.message: Summarize this PR and send it to Slack.

HOME.PainPoints.card1.solution.message: Read the diff, write the summary, post it to the target channel.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.title.message: Turn a Notion spec into Linear

HOME.PainPoints.card2.description.message: Keep docs, breakdown, and tracking in one chain.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.prompt.message: Read this Notion page, break it into tasks, and sync them to Linear.

HOME.PainPoints.card2.solution.message: Read the doc, split the tasks, write them back to Linear.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.title.message: Read a Gmail attachment, then call your API

HOME.PainPoints.card3.description.message: Put ready-made input and custom processing on one path.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.prompt.message: Read the Gmail attachment, call our PDF API, and return the result.

HOME.PainPoints.card3.solution.message: Take the email attachment, then keep going through your API.

HOME.ProofLayer.title.message: This path already runs in real products

HOME.ProofLayer.subtitle.message: OOMOL is not only about connecting a tool to an agent. The same capability path already carries into CLI, web tools, desktop apps, and cloud services.

HOME.ProofLayer.note.message: This layer now includes one live production example plus two placeholders. Later we can replace more of it with real services, team names, and usage data.

HOME.ProofLayer.signal1.description.message: This is not an internal demo. It is a live product that actively delivers PDF and publishing tools to end users.

HOME.ProofLayer.signal2.description.message: The same capability path already reaches web tools, desktop apps, and CLI instead of stopping at one demo page.

HOME.ProofLayer.signal3.description.message: These surfaces do not each rebuild a backend. They keep delivering through the same path.

HOME.ProofLayer.case1.description.message: This is a live web tool, not a showcase page. Users can upload PDFs and get EPUB or Markdown output through the same OOMOL delivery system.

HOME.ProofLayer.case2.description.message: The same project does not stop at browser conversion. It continues into a desktop app for managing a library and carrying those outputs into real usage.

HOME.ProofLayer.case3.description.message: Delivery does not end at file output. The result keeps moving into a real reading interface where it can be opened, browsed, and managed.

HOME.ValueProps.localCompute.title.message: Turn Local Compute Into Your Private AI Cloud

HOME.ValueProps.localCompute.description.message: Run workflows on your own PC or NAS. Leverage idle CPU/GPU resources, share them with others, or scale to OOMOL Cloud anytime.

HOME.ValueProps.workflowAPI.title.message: Develop With Workflows, Deliver as APIs

HOME.ValueProps.workflowAPI.description.message: Create function tools, assemble workflows, and publish them as REST or MCP APIs, with no extra server code required.

HOME.ValueProps.aiNative.description.message: AI-assisted function tool generation, vibe-coded workflows, and deep VSCode integration make development fast and flexible.

HOME.CoreFeatures.subtitle.message: This is not a new DSL. It is a delivery path that stays anchored in real code, uses AI to complete implementation, validates locally, and sends the same capability to different outputs.

HOME.CoreFeatures.workflow.title.message: 01｜Write the Function in a Real Codebase

HOME.CoreFeatures.workflow.description.message:
No new DSL, and no need to adapt to a platform before the work even starts.

• Keep writing real functions and project code
• Let AI complete implementation instead of inventing abstractions
• Reuse your existing libraries, scripts, and structure
• Make each function a delivery-ready unit

HOME.CoreFeatures.workflow.highlight1.message: Build the capability first, then decide where it should run

HOME.CoreFeatures.container.description.message:
Built-in containers keep dependencies, debugging, and runtime checks inside a local loop first.

• Verify inputs, outputs, logs, and dependencies locally
• Keep local behavior close to deployment behavior
• Lose less time to environment drift and rework

HOME.CoreFeatures.vscode.title.message: 03｜Ship to Cloud with the Same Implementation

HOME.CoreFeatures.vscode.description.message:
Send the function you already validated locally to OOMOL Cloud instead of building a second backend layer just to ship it.

• Publish directly as an API
• Publish directly as an MCP tool
• Publish directly as an automation task

HOME.CoreFeatures.vscode.highlight1.message: When the function is ready, delivery should follow naturally

HOME.Lifecycle.subtitle.message: From build to delivery, all-in-one development experience

HOME.Lifecycle.step1.subtitle.message: Design and implement workflows in OOMOL Studio

HOME.Lifecycle.step1.description.message: Create projects in OOMOL Studio, define functional nodes by installing ready-made function tools from the community or coding your own, then drag and drop to compose visual workflows with full AI assistance from design to production.

HOME.Lifecycle.step2.subtitle.message: Share with community, or manage as personal capability library

HOME.Lifecycle.step2.description.message: Choose to publish your packages publicly or privately — keep private packages to build your own modular capability library for reuse, or share them publicly in the community for others to benefit from your work.

HOME.Lifecycle.step3.subtitle.message: Run on local, private host or cloud

HOME.Lifecycle.step3.description.message: Deploy locally on your PC or Mac for personal use and sharing, or deploy to Cloud Serverless with one click for hassle-free API hosting.

HOME.Lifecycle.step4.subtitle.message: Transform into capabilities usable by both users and AI

HOME.Lifecycle.step4.description.message: Transform your workflows into external API services and interactive user applications, MCP Tools that AI can directly invoke, or automated workflows that run on schedules or triggers.

HOME.WhyOomol.subtitle.message: Compare with other tools and see how OOMOL redefines developer experience

HOME.PdfCraft.title.message: Open Source Project Built on OOMOL: PDF-Craft

HOME.PdfCraft.subtitle.message: Experience the power of local computing with our flagship open source project built on OOMOL

HOME.PdfCraft.description.message: PDF-Craft is OOMOL's official open source PDF to eBook conversion tool with 3000+ GitHub stars. Run it locally for free, share with friends via OOMOL, or use the official API service — there's always a service that fits you.

HOME.PdfCraft.feature2.message: Built on DeepSeek OCR, advanced model

HOME.PdfCraft.feature3.message: Professional team maintenance, open source and service in parallel

HOME.PdfCraft.traditional.step1.message: Prepare a PC with RTX 3060 or higher GPU

HOME.PdfCraft.traditional.step2.message: Install OOMOL Studio for free local deployment

HOME.PdfCraft.traditional.step3.message: Use Mini App or Flow to run

HOME.BrandAssets.subtitle.message: OOMOL Trademarks and Logos Must Not Be Modified or Used for Anything Other Than Representing OOMOL Inc.

HOME.Scenes.subtitle.message: From GPU computing sharing to AI Agent toolchains, explore oomol's powerful application scenarios

HOME.Scenes.gpu-sharing.inner.message: Share your RTX 4090 at home with your team remotely via FRP protocol. Supports NVIDIA GPU access through WSL2, enables P2P computing sharing, train models at night, share computing power during the day.

HOME.Scenes.mcp-server.inner.message: Develop custom MCP tools for Claude Desktop. Generate functions through visual workflows, automatically support MCP protocol, let AI Agents call your services directly.

HOME.Scenes.data-api.inner.message: Turn local Python scripts into API services with one click. Combine pandas, numpy and other data analysis libraries, package with containers, automatically generate RESTful API interfaces.

HOME.Scenes.ai-image-service.inner.message: Deploy Stable Diffusion inference service. Utilize local GPU computing power, provide image generation API externally through FRP, support multi-model switching and custom parameters.

HOME.Scenes.iot-control.inner.message: Remotely control Raspberry Pi and IoT devices through FRP. Build workflows to orchestrate smart home, achieve remote monitoring, data collection and automation control.

HOME.Scenes.agent-toolchain.inner.message: Combine multiple MCP Tools to build expert Agents. Integrate capabilities like data query, file processing, API calls to create domain-specific intelligent assistants.

HOME.Scenes.data-science.inner.message: Create powerful AI tools for video processing, including automated transcription, translation, subtitling, and content analysis using advanced AI models and intuitive visual workflows.

HOME.Scenes.media-processing.inner.message: A multimedia editing toolkit powered by FFmpeg, supporting video editing, format conversion, audio processing, and more.

HOME.Scenes.ai-image-edit.inner.message: Edit and transform images with text instructions using AI models. Easily change objects, adjust scenes, or add creative effects.

HOME.Scenes.ai-vibe-coding.inner.message: With AI Vibe Coding, just describe what you need — AI will automatically generate complete workflows and nodes, letting you build powerful AI tools quickly and easily, no programming required.

HOME.Scenes.drag-drop-manual.inner.message: Easily build custom workflows with a simple drag-and-drop interface, giving you full control over every node and connection — ideal for creating tailored AI tools.

HOME.Scenes.text-processing.inner.message: Intelligent text analysis and processing, including sentiment analysis, keyword extraction, text summarization, translation, and content generation for efficient document workflows.

HOME.Scenes.zip-archive-manipulation.inn.message: A professional ZIP archive toolkit covering compression, extraction, encryption, encoding conversion, and a variety of ZIP file operations.

HOME.Scenes.txt-to-epub.inner.message: Convert TXT files into beautifully formatted EPUB eBooks.

HOME.Scenes.pdf-processing.inner.message: A comprehensive PDF toolkit that supports merging, splitting, OCR, form filling, security management, and more.

HOME.Scenes.data-analysis.inner.message: Analyze tabular data with AI and generate customized charts based on your needs.

HOME.Scenes.audio-processing.inner.message: Professional audio processing tools supporting speech recognition, audio enhancement, noise reduction, music generation, and sound analysis for complete audio solutions.

HOME.Scenes.automation.inner.message: Build intelligent automation workflows connecting various applications and services for automated task execution, data synchronization, and business process optimization.

HOME.Scenes.web-scraping.inner.message: Intelligent web data extraction tools supporting dynamic content retrieval, data cleaning, and structured storage for easy access to web data.

HOME.Scenes.api-integration.inner.message: Seamlessly connect various third-party services and APIs for data exchange, feature integration, and business collaboration to build powerful integration solutions.

HOME.Scenes.content-generation.inner.message: AI-powered content creation tools supporting article writing, image generation, video production, and creative design to unleash unlimited creative potential.

HOME.Create.title.message: From Creation to Deployment, All-in-One Development Experience

HOME.Create.subtitle.message: Create Workflow → Generate Package → Publish to Community → Flexible Deployment (Local FRP / Cloud API)

HOME.Products.subtitle.message: Explore the three core products in the OOMOL ecosystem, covering the complete AI tool lifecycle from creation to sharing to usage.

HOME.Products.studio.description.message: OOMOL's AI tool creation platform. The core advantage lies in: retaining the unlimited creative ability of traditional programming while having the convenience of low-code development, allowing users to efficiently build various AI tools.

HOME.Products.hub.description.message: AI tool sharing community. The best platform to discover, share, and get inspired.

HOME.Products.chat.description.message: Intelligent conversation tool. Use various AI features through natural language interaction.

HOME.CommunityShare.title.message: A function you wrote once should keep paying off

HOME.CommunityShare.subtitle.message: A function should not stay trapped in one project. After publishing, it can come back into local work, plug into AI workflows, or become the base capability behind future services.

HOME.CommunityShare.point1.description.message: Bring published functions back into your own environment so you do not have to start from scratch the next time.

HOME.CommunityShare.point2.description.message: Each release leaves behind reusable capabilities that gradually become your own function library.

HOME.CommunityShare.point3.title.message: Compose New Services from Existing Ones

HOME.CommunityShare.point3.description.message: Keep extending published functions and recombining them into new products and services.

HOME.Features.subtitle.message: Everything you need to create, customize, and share AI tools - no coding required.

HOME.Features.intuitive-interaction.inne.message: Build AI tools with simple drag-and-drop. No coding knowledge required.

HOME.Features.pre-installed-environment..message: Everything is pre-configured. Start creating AI tools immediately.

HOME.Features.programming-friendly.inner.message: Download and run. No complex setup or dependency management.

HOME.Features.support-sharing.inner.message: Share your AI tools with one click. Others can use them instantly.

HOME.Features.block-sharing.inner.message: Build once, use everywhere. Create reusable AI components for the community.

HOME.Ecosystem.subtitle.message: From local development to cloud deployment, complete AI tool lifecycle

HOME.Ecosystem.studio.description.message: VSCode-integrated visual editor with AI-assisted development and local container runtime.

HOME.Ecosystem.hub.description.message: Open package sharing community, publish, browse and install Blocks, one-click dependency integration.

HOME.Ecosystem.cloud.description.message: One-click deployment to cloud, providing RESTful and MCP API protocols, with metering and billing support.

HOME.Ecosystem.chat.description.message: AI chat assistant based on MCP Tools, combine Tools to build expert Agents, multi-model support.

HOME.Guide.subtitle.message: Build AI tool ecosystem with global developers, share experience, get support, and contribute to open source.

HOME.Guide.github.description.message: Participate in open source projects, contribute code, report issues, and collaborate with developers worldwide.

HOME.Guide.discord.description.message: Real-time communication, get technical support, share experiences, and meet like-minded developers.

HOME.Guide.docs.description.message: Complete API docs, sample code, best practices, get started with oomol development quickly.

HOME.Guide.project-1.description.message: AI-powered video translation and subtitle generation

HOME.Guide.project-8.description.message: Extract and structure data from documents with AI

HOME.Guide.project-10.description.message: Various feature sets of the SiliconFlow platform

HOME.Built-in.subtitle.message: Use a single OOMOL API Token to quickly access various common APIs and mainstream LLMs. Simpler, more unified development experience.

HOME.Built-in.code.python.message:
from oocana import Context

#region generated meta
import typing
Inputs = typing.Dict[str, typing.Any]
Outputs = typing.Dict[str, typing.Any]
#endregion

async def main(params: Inputs, context: Context) -> Outputs | None:

    # Fusion API Base URL
    api_url = "https://fusion-api.oomol.com/v1/fal-nano-banana-edit/submit"

    # LLM Base URL, can use openai sdk
    llm_base_url = "https://llm.oomol.com/v1"

    # Get OOMOL token from context (no need for manual API key input)
    api_token = await context.oomol_token()

    return {
      # "output": "output_value"
    }

HOME.GetStartedPrompt.title.message: Start with ready-made tools, build your own only when needed

HOME.GetStartedPrompt.subtitle.message: Use oo-cli to get ready-made tools working first. When you need custom ones, move into Studio and Cloud to compose, extend, and deliver your own tools.

HOME.Downloads.subtitle.message: Start by installing oo-cli so agents can connect to SaaS tools and published capabilities. When you need to extend and customize your own tools, download OOMOL Studio to build and validate them locally.

HOME.Downloads.studioSection.subtitle.message: Use Studio when ready-made tools stop short. Build, extend, and validate your own tools locally before you continue into Cloud.

HOME.Downloads.Canary.subtitle.message: Versions With New Features But Not Fully Tested

HOME.Downloads.cli.info.description.message: Use oo-cli to connect your Agent to SaaS tools and published capabilities first. You can install it in your terminal or send the prompt below to your Agent and let it handle the setup and verification.

HOME.Downloads.cli.subtitle.message: Install oo-cli in Codex, Claude Code, or any local terminal so Agents can connect to SaaS integrations and published capabilities in OOMOL, then search, invoke, and get results directly.

HOME.Downloads.cli.method.install.descri.message: Run these commands in Codex, Claude Code, or any local terminal, then sign in and start using oo-cli.

HOME.Downloads.cli.method.agent.descript.message: Paste this prompt to your agent if you want it to install oo-cli and verify the setup for you.

HOME.Downloads.cli.method.agent.prompt.message: Install oo-cli globally with Bun by running `bun install -g @oomol-lab/oo-cli`, then run `oo login`, confirm the CLI is ready, and tell me the next oo-cli command to use.

HOME.Downloads.cta.title.message: Easy to install, easy to extend

HOME.Downloads.cta.description.message: Use oo-cli to get a ready-made tool working first. When you need custom logic, come back and download OOMOL Studio.

HOME.Community.subtitle.message: Get updates, send direct feedback, and follow the open-source projects we keep maintaining.

HOME.Community.slogan-1.message: See product and release updates early

HOME.Community.slogan-2.message: Share problems and real usage scenarios directly

HOME.Community.slogan-3.message: Track the open-source projects we maintain

HOME.Community.slogan-4.message: Every suggestion feeds product and docs improvements

HOME.Community.open-source.subtitle.message: These projects carry OOMOL's ongoing work on runtime, deployment, and real-world developer tools.

FEATURES.title.message: All the features that you need

FEATURES.description.message: Easy drag-and-drop workflows, professional code editor, and elegant data visualization.

FEATURES.sceneOne.content-1.message: Build workflows with simple drag-and-drop function tools to fit your needs.

FEATURES.sceneOne.content-2.message: Automatically offer UI components based on parameter types for easier use.

FEATURES.sceneOne.content-3.message: An intuitive UI helps users clearly understand the system’s status.

FEATURES.sceneOne.content-4.message: Readable logs with clear sequence and explicit status.

FEATURES.sceneTwo.content-5.message: Built-in Python and Node.js with support for open-source library installation.

FEATURES.sceneTwo.content-6.message: Based on Visual Studio Code with code completion, highlighting, and AI tips.

FEATURES.sceneTwo.content-7.message: Each node is a function—customize them easily if you know programming.

FEATURES.block-1.title.message: Containerized development environment for seamless cross-system workflow sharing.

FEATURES.block-2.title.message: Protect local environment and data security through secure isolation.

FEATURES.block-3.title.message: Unified Linux environment, easy image deployment to cloud.

FEATURES.block-4.title.message: Share workflow templates for easy scenario application and secondary development.

FEATURES.block-5.title.message: Share function tool libraries with connectable, ready-to-use functions.

FEATURES.block-6.title.message: Code synced to GitHub for collaborative development and upkeep.

PRICING.subtitle.message: Build locally for free. Subscribe only when you want OOMOL to host delivery and managed usage.

PRICING.model.summary.message: OOMOL Studio is free for local development. Paid plans start when you want to publish online, run managed tasks, or use hosted AI services.

PRICING.model.local.title.message: Build and validate in Studio for free

PRICING.model.local.text.message: Use the full local authoring environment, test with your own dependencies, and bring your own model service when needed.

PRICING.model.online.title.message: Subscribe when you want to ship and host

PRICING.model.online.text.message: Use Pro or Max when you want OOMOL to take on the online delivery layer for the same validated function as an API, MCP tool, or automation task.

PRICING.subscription.subtitle.message: Free covers local development and hosted trials. Pro and Max are for online publishing, hosted runs, and predictable Cloud Task costs.

PRICING.subscription.free.description.message:
For local development,
validation, and everyday work
with your own model services

PRICING.subscription.free.feature1.message: Full OOMOL Studio local development environment

PRICING.subscription.free.feature2.message: Bring your own model service when you do not want managed AI billing

PRICING.subscription.free.feature3.message: No charge for local runs inside Studio

PRICING.subscription.free.feature4.message: 200 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.free.feature5.message: 1 sign-up credit to try LLM and Fusion API usage

PRICING.subscription.free.feature6.message: Publish up to 20 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.subscription.pro.description.message:
For publishing validated functions
as APIs, MCP tools, or automation tasks,
with OOMOL-managed hosting

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature1.message: Publish online with OOMOL-managed delivery and runtime

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature2.message: 1,800 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature3.message: $3 in general credits included each month

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature4.message: Built for moving from local validation to hosted delivery

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature5.message: Top up at the standard rate after the included allowance

PRICING.subscription.pro.feature6.message: Publish up to 100 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.subscription.max.description.message:
For steady online traffic that needs
a larger monthly hosted allowance
for ongoing hosted runs

PRICING.subscription.max.feature1.message: All Pro features, with higher limits

PRICING.subscription.max.feature2.message: 14,400 Cloud Task minutes included each month

PRICING.subscription.max.feature3.message: $8 in general credits included each month

PRICING.subscription.max.feature4.message: Better fit for steady high-volume hosted runs

PRICING.subscription.max.feature5.message: Top up at the standard rate after the included allowance

PRICING.subscription.max.feature6.message: Publish up to 300 packages total, public and private combined

PRICING.payAsYouGo.subtitle.message: Pay only for what you use, flexible and transparent

PRICING.payAsYouGo.taskExecution.descrip.message: Billing for workflow and tool executions

PRICING.payAsYouGo.llm.description.message: Billing for mainstream LLM API calls

PRICING.tables.subtitle.message: When you use managed AI, Cloud Task, or hosted service calls, usage is billed in credits with the same rates shown in OOMOL Console.

PRICING.tables.syncNote.message: LLM pricing is fetched live from console. Cloud Task and Fusion API rows reflect the current console table. Pro is designed to give you included usage plus a better price when you keep growing.

PRICING.tables.llm.note.message: LLM rates update with the model configuration in console. Units are credits per M token.

PRICING.tables.llm.loading.message: Loading the latest LLM pricing from OOMOL Console...

PRICING.tables.llm.error.message: LLM pricing is temporarily unavailable. Please refresh later or check console directly.

PRICING.tables.llm.empty.message: No LLM pricing data is available right now.

PRICING.tables.cloudTask.noteText.message: Anything under one minute is billed as one minute

PRICING.tables.cloudTask.alert.message: Running tools locally in OOMOL Studio does not trigger Cloud Task billing. Running tools in Chat or hub.oomol.com does.

PRICING.tables.fusionApi.alert.message: Some official tools provided by OOMOL use these services. Users can also call these APIs directly, and credits are deducted on invocation.

PRICING.tables.fusionApi.description.nan.message: Nano Banana image generation or editing
Nano Banana Pro image generation or editing

PRICING.free.subtitle.message: Run Studio locally, share via FRP protocol, forever free

PRICING.standard.subtitle.message: Suitable For Individual Users, Providing Credits And Support.

PRICING.question.subtitle.message: If You Have Any Other Questions, Please

PRICING.question.text1.answer.message: The Free Credits Gifted Each Month Are Only Valid Within That Month, But Purchased Credits Package Does Not Expire, It Is Unavailable Only When It Is Used Up

PRICING.question.text2.message: How Does OOMOL Prioritize Credit Usage?

PRICING.question.text2.answer.message: Prioritize The Use Of Free Credits

CREATE.description.message: OOMOL's AI tool creation platform. The core advantage lies in: retaining the unlimited creative ability of traditional programming while having the convenience of low-code development, allowing users to efficiently build various AI tools.

STUDIO.hero.description.message: Local development environment for developers, VSCode-based visual programming tool with FRP protocol for P2P computing power sharing

STUDIO.painPoints.title.message: Three Pain Points of Traditional Workflow Tools

STUDIO.painPoints.subtitle.message: As developers, we know exactly how frustrating these problems are

STUDIO.painPoints.learningCurve.descript.message: Learning the platform's interaction and design concepts, simple if-else logic becomes complicated with drag-and-drop components. Problems that could be solved with one line of code require time to understand the platform's abstract concepts.

STUDIO.painPoints.extension.description.message: Problems that could be solved by importing an open-source library in programming become as complex as rocket science in workflow platforms. Want to extend functionality? Check if the platform supports it first—if not, you're stuck.

STUDIO.painPoints.toolSwitching.descript.message: Even if the platform provides scripting, you have to write code in a clunky web editor instead of your familiar VSCode. You lose code completion, debugging tools, and various extensions, significantly reducing development efficiency.

STUDIO.solutions.subtitle.message: We redesigned the workflow development experience based on developers' actual needs

STUDIO.solutions.functions.title.message: Nodes as Functions, Zero Barrier for Libraries

STUDIO.solutions.functions.description.message: In OOMOL Studio, nodes are functions. You don't need to learn any new concepts—write code in familiar ways and import any open-source library. Need a feature? Just npm install.

STUDIO.solutions.functions.highlight.message: No new concepts, no ceiling. Create workflows like writing code.

STUDIO.solutions.vscode.title.message: Built on VSCode, Inherit Your Coding Habits

STUDIO.solutions.vscode.description.message: OOMOL Studio is built on VSCode, so you can write node code in your familiar editor. Code completion, debugging tools, Git integration, various extensions... all your familiar development experience is here.

STUDIO.solutions.vscode.highlight.message: Familiar editor, familiar shortcuts, zero adaptation cost.

STUDIO.solutions.container.title.message: Built-in Container, Consistent Environment for Easy Deployment

STUDIO.solutions.container.description.message: Built-in container environment ensures local development is identical to server environment. Write code and run it directly without worrying about 'works on my machine'. Network proxy turns your device into a personal server, zero-cost task launching.

STUDIO.solutions.container.highlight.message: Local is production, no deployment pitfalls. Idle devices can serve too.

STUDIO.hero.description.old.message: Generate functions with full code, validate them locally, and carry them forward into service delivery.

STUDIO.workflow.subtitle.message: Create Workflow → Generate Package → Publish to Community → Flexible Deployment

STUDIO.workflow.step1.description.message: Drag-and-drop visual editor, VSCode-based function tool coding

STUDIO.workflow.step2.description.message: Function tools + Flows auto-packaging, MCP interface support

STUDIO.workflow.step2.feature2.message: MCP interface support (AI Agent direct access)

STUDIO.workflow.step3.description.message: One-click publish workflow templates and function tool libraries

STUDIO.workflow.step3.feature2.message: Share function tool libraries to the community

STUDIO.workflow.step4.description.message: Local deployment + FRP sharing, or one-click cloud deployment

STUDIO.advantages.vscode.description.message: Professional code editor with vibe-coded function tools and AI-assisted development

STUDIO.advantages.vscode.feature3.message: Nodes as functions, seamless code-visual switching

STUDIO.advantages.p2p.description.message: FRP protocol-based local deployment sharing, P2P-like computing sharing

STUDIO.advantages.p2p.feature1.message: Local deployment via FRP protocol sharing

STUDIO.advantages.p2p.feature3.message: Use privately or share with others

STUDIO.advantages.api.description.message: Low-cost local function publishing as API service, MCP interface for AI Agent access

STUDIO.advantages.api.feature1.message: Low-cost local function publishing as API

STUDIO.advantages.api.feature2.message: MCP interface for AI Agent access

STUDIO.ai.description.message: Integrate mainstream AI services with rich AI capability support

STUDIO.ai.feature1.message: Multiple large language models (GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.)

STUDIO.ai.feature2.message: Image generation and processing (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion)

STUDIO.case.title.message: Case Study: Building PDF-Craft with Studio

STUDIO.case.subtitle.message: Complete workflow from function tool development to API publishing with OOMOL Studio

STUDIO.case.step1.desc.message: Develop core function tools for PDF parsing, OCR recognition, and format conversion in VSCode

STUDIO.case.step2.desc.message: Connect function tools through drag-and-drop to build a complete PDF processing workflow

STUDIO.case.step3.desc.message: Auto-generate RESTful API and MCP interface for AI Agent integration

STUDIO.case.deploy.local.desc.message: Run and debug locally, completely free

STUDIO.case.deploy.frp.desc.message: Share with team members via FRP protocol

STUDIO.case.deploy.cloud.desc.message: One-click deploy to OOMOL Cloud with auto-scaling

CREATE.workflow.description.message: Build complex AI workflows through drag-and-drop interface, no programming experience required

CREATE.workflow.feature1.message: Intuitive drag-and-drop interface, easily connect various AI modules and functional components

CREATE.workflow.feature2.message: Rich preset templates, quickly start common AI application scenarios

CREATE.workflow.feature3.message: Real-time preview and debugging, instantly view workflow execution results

CREATE.workflow.feature4.message: Version control and collaboration, support team development and maintenance of workflows

CREATE.ai.description.message: Integrate mainstream AI services, providing rich AI capability support

CREATE.ai.feature1.message: Support multiple large language models, including GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.

CREATE.ai.feature2.message: Image generation and processing capabilities, support DALL-E, Midjourney and other services

CREATE.ai.feature3.message: Speech recognition and synthesis, enabling multimodal AI interaction experience

SHARE.description.message: Turn finished capabilities into installable, reusable, and composable assets for the team.

SHARE.hub.description.message: Install, reuse, and extend function assets from a shared team catalog.

SHARE.flowCommunity.description.message: Keep capabilities in one catalog so the team knows what exists, how to install it, and who maintains it.

SHARE.flowCommunity.feature1.message: Turn finished functions into installable assets instead of leaving them buried in projects

SHARE.flowCommunity.feature2.message: Search existing capabilities by name and purpose to avoid rebuilding the same thing

SHARE.flowCommunity.feature3.message: Track versions and ownership so the team knows which capability to reuse

SHARE.flowCommunity.feature4.message: Keep composing on top of existing assets until they grow into larger services

SHARE.analytics.description.message: Know not only what was published, but what is actually installed, called, and maintained over time.

SHARE.analytics.feature1.message: See which assets are installed and called most often to judge reuse value

SHARE.analytics.feature2.message: Use adoption signals to improve parameters, dependencies, and delivery paths

SHARE.analytics.feature3.message: Help maintainers decide which capabilities are worth extending further

USE.description.message: Enjoy smooth AI tool usage experience through intelligent chat tools

USE.chatTools.description.message: Easily use various AI tools through intuitive chat interface, no complex operations required

USE.chatTools.feature1.message: Natural language interaction, use AI tools like chatting

USE.chatTools.feature2.message: Multi-turn conversation support, maintain contextual coherence

USE.chatTools.feature3.message: Smart prompts and suggestions, help users better use tools

USE.chatTools.feature4.message: History management, review and reuse conversation content anytime

USE.userExperience.description.message: Provide smooth and efficient AI tool usage experience for users

USE.userExperience.feature1.message: Responsive design, support seamless switching between desktop and mobile

USE.userExperience.feature2.message: Real-time feedback and status display, let users understand processing progress

USE.userExperience.feature3.message: Personalized settings, customize usage interface according to user preferences

CHAT.hero.description.message: MCP Tool-based AI chat assistant, build expert Agents by combining Tools

CHAT.useCases.subtitle.message: Complete AI tasks through natural conversation

CHAT.useCase.conversation.description.message: Use AI tools like chatting, multi-turn conversations maintain context coherence

CHAT.useCase.tools.description.message: Call MCP Tools created in Studio to achieve complex feature combinations

CHAT.useCase.knowledge.description.message: Integrate enterprise knowledge to build domain-specific intelligent assistants

CHAT.useCase.collaboration.description.message: Share conversations and tool configurations to improve team collaboration efficiency

CHAT.features.mcp.description.message: Native MCP protocol support, directly call various tools

CHAT.features.agent.description.message: Build domain-specific Agents by combining multiple Tools

CHAT.features.models.description.message: Support mainstream AI models, freely switch and choose

CHAT.features.models.feature1.message: GPT, Claude, Gemini and other mainstream models

HOME.Scenes.dataScience.inner.message: Build end-to-end pipelines from data cleaning to model training to visualization, supporting pandas, scikit-learn, and GPU-accelerated deep learning.

HOME.Scenes.multimedia.inner.message: Batch video transcoding, auto multi-language subtitles, PDF generation. Leverage ffmpeg, Pillow and more to build efficient media processing pipelines.

HOME.Scenes.aiAgent.inner.message: Convert function tools into AI-callable tools via MCP protocol, composing specialized agents. Integrate data querying, file processing, API calls and more.

HOME.Scenes.apiPublish.inner.message: Package workflows as RESTful APIs or Chatbot interfaces, deploy to OOMOL Cloud with one click, auto-generating API docs and test UI.

HOME.Scenes.enterprise.inner.message: Containerized deployment to private cloud or K8s clusters, supporting CI/CD pipelines for continuous delivery and operations of enterprise-grade API services.

HOME.ProductComparison.title.message: Cloud is where a tool becomes deliverable

HOME.ProductComparison.subtitle.message: OOMOL Cloud is not just runtime. It is the delivery layer that turns a validated implementation into a deliverable tool you can provide privately to yourself, your team, or your customers.

HOME.ChatAgent.description.message: OOMOL Chat is the agent interface powered by your own function tools. Every function tool, workflow, or API becomes an MCP tool the agent can call.

HOME.ProductComparison.studio.scenario.message: Best for development, function debugging, and local validation before deciding how far delivery should go.

HOME.ProductComparison.studio.tech.message: Install locally and use immediately, with a built-in container environment that keeps development and validation in one place.

HOME.ProductComparison.cloud.capability.message: Deploy validated tools to Cloud so users get the capability without receiving your source code.

HOME.ProductComparison.cloud.scenario.message: For developers and small teams that want to share, sell, or operate reusable tools without rebuilding a separate SaaS around each one.

HOME.ProductComparison.cloud.tech.message: Reuse the implementation you already validated in Studio while OOMOL Cloud handles runtime, access, and external delivery. When needed, the same tool can still expand through API or MCP.

HOME.ProductComparison.outputSummary.message: The same validated implementation can be consumed through terminal, apps, agents, and automation without splitting into separate products.

HOME.ProductComparison.output1.descripti.message: Search, inspect, and run deployed tools directly through oo-cli and agent terminal workflows.

HOME.ProductComparison.output2.descripti.message: Carry the same tool into your own application surfaces, backend services, or product integrations.

HOME.ProductComparison.output3.descripti.message: Expose the same tool to agent ecosystems or keep it running on schedules and triggers.

HOME.ProductComparison.benefit1.value.message: Ship the tool, keep the code

HOME.ProductComparison.benefit1.descript.message: Cloud lets you provide the capability without shipping the implementation, so distribution does not force you into open-sourcing or rebuilding the product as SaaS.

HOME.ProductComparison.benefit2.descript.message: Stay focused on the tool itself instead of rebuilding auth, hosting, scheduling, and interface layers around the same implementation.

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.title.message: Get one tool working first, then decide whether to build your own

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.subtitle.message: If you just want to get moving, start with oo-cli. When ready-made tools stop short, move into Studio + Cloud.

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card1.title.message: Let agents use a ready-made tool from the terminal first

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card1.description.message: If you want Codex, OpenClaw, or Claude Code working first, install oo-cli and start by searching, inspecting, and running published tools.

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card1.note.message: Fastest way to get one tool working.

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card2.valueCaptio.message: Enter when you need your own tools

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card2.title.message: When you need your own APIs and logic, move into Studio + Cloud

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card2.description.message: When ready-made tools stop short, use Studio + Cloud to add your APIs, internal logic, and long-running delivery path.

HOME.DeveloperBenefits.card2.note.message: Best when you need custom tools and continuous delivery.

HOME.CliEntry.title.message: oo-cli is the default way to discover and run tools

HOME.CliEntry.subtitle.message: Install once in Codex, Claude Code, or any terminal. Search packages, inspect inputs, run tools, and get results before you wire API or MCP.

HOME.CliEntry.benefit1.description.message: Search, inspect, and run tools other developers have already published instead of building your own path on day one.

HOME.CliEntry.benefit2.description.message: When existing tools are not enough, use Studio to generate, validate, and publish your own tools through Cloud.

HOME.CliEntry.bridge.message: Publish the function to OOMOL Cloud first. Then let CLI handle sign-in, discovery, and execution. That relationship only needs to be stated once.

HOME.CliEntry.step1.description.message: Install the default entry point for using published tools.

HOME.CliEntry.step2.description.message: Sign in, then start searching and running published tools.

HOME.CliEntry.step3.description.message: Check what the tool does and what input it needs, then run it directly from the terminal.

HOME.CliEntry.setup.title.message: Two commands to install and sign in to oo-cli

HOME.CliEntry.setup.note.message: Install once, then use it directly.

HOME.CliEntry.demo.title.message: Reduce the first run to three steps

HOME.CliEntry.demo.note.message: Show the first successful path directly.

HOME.CliEntry.demo.stage1.description.message: Search before memorizing package names. That matches how new users actually approach CLI.

HOME.CliEntry.demo.stage2.description.message: Check package info before running so you do not guess the block-id or parameters.

HOME.CliEntry.demo.stage3.description.message: Cloud executes the function. CLI is the layer that sends the task and pulls the result back into the current workflow.

HOME.CliEntry.demo.footer.message: The same published function can still be reused later as an API, MCP tool, or automation task.

HOME.CliEntry.terminal.note.message: CLI is not just another entry point. After sign-in, it is the layer that connects cloud functions deployed on OOMOL Cloud to Codex, Claude Code, and other terminal workflows in a stable way.

HOME.CodexBlocks.title.message: Start with representative tools in Codex

HOME.CodexBlocks.subtitle.message: Instead of the old proof section, show the kinds of tools that better define OOMOL's capability surface. Search, inspect, and run them in Codex first, then decide whether you need to build your own.

HOME.CodexBlocks.card1.description.message: Call a tool from Codex to translate a full comic page, clean text, reflow layout, and return a readable result for serialized pages or promo art.

HOME.CodexBlocks.card1.previewNote.message: This area will be replaced with video to show the full path from a Codex call to the translated output.

HOME.CodexBlocks.card2.description.message: Generate paper-style figures from method descriptions, notes, or rough sketches for papers, slides, and technical writing.

HOME.CodexBlocks.card2.previewNote.message: This area will be replaced with video to show the full path from method description to generated paper figure.

HOME.CodexBlocks.card3.description.message: Translate long-form documents into bilingual EPUB or Markdown outputs that stay usable for reading instead of returning only raw text.

CLOUD.hero.description.message: Take the tool you already validated locally and run it in the cloud, so users can use the capability without getting your source code or a custom service stack.

CLOUD.hero.note.message: Users get the tool through oo-cli, apps, or agents. Your implementation stays on the server.

CLOUD.painPoints.title.message: Shipping a tool should not mean rebuilding a SaaS

CLOUD.painPoints.subtitle.message: We know the struggle as developers

CLOUD.painPoints.description.message: What slows delivery is usually not the tool itself, but the work around it: packaging a user surface, hosting it reliably, handling access, and keeping operations stable. Cloud is built to compress that layer.

CLOUD.solution.subtitle.message: The same tool can be consumed through API, MCP, or automation while staying backed by one implementation.

CLOUD.modes.library.description.message: Run the same tool on triggers or schedules so it keeps delivering value beyond local and manual execution.

CLOUD.modes.serverless.description.message: Give applications direct API access to the same tool without rebuilding a separate backend surface.

CLOUD.modes.mcp.description.message: Expose the same tool to agents and AI apps as an MCP tool when agent-native integration matters.

CLOUD.pricing.title.message: Start small, pay when the tool is used

CLOUD.pricing.description.message: You do not need dedicated servers just to test distribution. Publish the tool first, validate demand, and pay as usage grows.

CLOUD.opensource.subtitle.message: The runtime foundation stays open source even when the tool you publish stays private.

CLOUD.opensource.description.message: OOMOL's virtual machine and runtime core stay open source, so you keep deployment freedom across personal servers, private infrastructure, or public cloud environments while choosing how each tool is delivered.

CLOUD.opensource.ovm.description.message: Cross-platform virtual machine manager providing unified container runtime for OOMOL

CLOUD.opensource.ovm-core.description.message: Core runtime library for OVM, handling container lifecycle and resource management

CLOUD.opensource.ovm-win.description.message: OVM implementation for Windows platform, supporting WSL2 and Hyper-V

CLOUD.opensource.ovm-mac.description.message: OVM implementation for macOS platform, based on Apple Virtualization Framework

APP.new.hero.description.message: A single cloud skill can be delivered through chat or structured applets, without building separate product surfaces from scratch.

APP.new.concept.description.message: OOMOL turns the same cloud skill into usable entry points. You focus on the implementation; we connect it to the user.

APP.new.chat.description.message: When user intent is still vague, let AI choose and run the right cloud skill through conversation.

APP.new.chat.meta1.value.message: Best when a cloud skill should be used through conversation and intent needs clarification first

APP.new.chat.meta2.value.message: Understand intent first, then call the right cloud skill with less setup

APP.new.chat.devValue.message: Cloud skill as agent tool: the same implementation becomes an AI-callable surface.

APP.new.applet.description.message: When inputs are clear, run the same cloud skill through structured parameters for more reliable delivery.

APP.new.applet.meta1.value.message: Best for teams delivering fixed-input cloud skills to internal or external users

APP.new.applet.meta2.value.message: Wrap the same cloud skill as a fillable, executable, and reusable entry point

APP.new.applet.devValue.message: Cloud skill as app: auto-generated parameter UI with less frontend boilerplate.

STUDIO.page.title.message: OOMOL Studio - Let AI Agents Build Tools Automatically

STUDIO.page.description.message: Let an AI agent build the tool automatically first, then verify, revise, and ship the same code in a real coding environment.

STUDIO.manifesto.title.message: The Studio for Building Agent Tools

STUDIO.manifesto.subtitle.message: Let an AI agent build the tool automatically first, then verify, revise, and call it directly through oo-cli in a real engineering environment.

STUDIO.product.story.title.message:
Tool Extension,
Really Comes Down to Three Problems

STUDIO.product.story.description.message:
Whether you compose with workflows or build function services, you usually get blocked by:
not being able to let an AI agent build automatically, DSL learning cost, and extension cost.

STUDIO.product.principles.title.message:
OOMOL Studio gets these three things
right in one pass

STUDIO.product.principles.description.message: Start by letting AI handle the work. When manual adjustment or deeper tuning is needed, you can step in directly with familiar coding skills instead of learning a DSL.

STUDIO.product.cta.title.message: Start with your next function tool

STUDIO.product.cta.description.message: Use a real coding environment to let an AI agent generate, debug, and validate function tools. When you are ready to deliver, carry them forward through Cloud.

STUDIO.story.paragraph1.message: What we want is not another tool that asks developers to adapt to the platform, but a working environment where an AI agent can build automatically first, and engineers can keep writing functions, orchestrating nodes, debugging dependencies, and validating results.

STUDIO.story.frustration.line1.message: "Why can't I just let an AI agent build this tool automatically?"

STUDIO.story.frustration.line2.message: "Why do I still have to learn a proprietary DSL before I can compose anything?"

STUDIO.story.frustration.line3.message: "Why do I still have to wait for platform support just to import a library?"

STUDIO.story.paragraph2.message: For developers, the real problem is not whether something can be dragged visually. It is whether the first step from requirement to usable tool can be completed automatically by an AI agent.

STUDIO.story.paragraph3.message: So Studio has a clear role: put fully automatic building by an AI agent into a real engineering environment, then connect local validation and delivery.

STUDIO.story.point1.title.message: You have to learn a DSL before you can even compose

STUDIO.story.point1.description.message: Many platforms build workflows on top of proprietary low-code syntax. Before you extend anything, you first learn the platform.

STUDIO.story.point2.title.message: You can compose, but dependencies and extension stay awkward

STUDIO.story.point2.description.message: Connecting nodes does not mean the system is truly extensible. Importing libraries or changing logic still gets stuck on platform constraints.

STUDIO.story.point3.title.message: You still cannot let an AI agent build it automatically

STUDIO.story.point3.description.message: The AI agent should find packages and nodes, connect them, and generate new nodes when needed.

HOME.StudioChain.title.message: From idea to a deliverable tool

HOME.StudioChain.subtitle.message: When ready-made tools are not enough, OOMOL gives you one continuous path: generate in Studio, validate locally, publish through Cloud, and deliver through oo-cli.

HOME.StudioChain.lead.message: Do not stop at local automation. Build a tool people can actually use.

HOME.StudioChain.summary.message: In OOMOL, you can start with ready-made tools, create your own implementation in Studio, validate it locally, then publish it privately to yourself, your team, or your customers.

HOME.StudioChain.card1.description.message: Describe, write, debug, and validate your own tools in a real coding environment for code, dependencies, and inputs.

HOME.StudioChain.card2.description.message: Publish the validated implementation as a tool through Cloud and keep serving it without exposing your source code.

Theme.Navbar.product.studio.description.message: Compose and generate functions to extend oo-cli

Theme.Navbar.product.cli.description.message: Install in many agents and use tools directly

Theme.Navbar.product.cloud.description.message: Run, manage, and automate tools in the cloud

Theme.Navbar.product.ai.description.message: Use the official GUI agent for the same tools

STUDIO.principle1.title.message: 1. Let the AI agent build it automatically

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph1.message: Describe the tool you want, and the AI agent can search for the right packages and nodes to build the tool automatically.

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph2.message: When the existing nodes are not enough, it can keep going by generating new function nodes instead of only completing a few lines of code.

STUDIO.principle1.paragraph3.message: From requirement to a usable tool, the first step is to let the AI agent build it automatically.

STUDIO.principle2.title.message: 2. Start composing without learning a DSL first

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph1.message: In Studio, nodes are still functions. Inputs are arguments. Outputs are return values.

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph2.message: When you describe, review, or edit a flow, you work with concepts developers already understand instead of translating ideas into a platform DSL.

STUDIO.principle2.paragraph3.message: That keeps learning cost out of the critical path and makes orchestration understandable from the start.

STUDIO.principle3.title.message: 3. Extend it the way engineers actually do

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph1.message: Install, import, and validate the dependencies you need in a real engineering environment instead of waiting for a platform allowlist.

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph2.message: Changing logic, adding nodes, and connecting external capabilities all happen in the same codebase and container.

STUDIO.principle3.paragraph3.message: So the flow is not just composable on the surface. It can keep expanding with the rest of your stack.

SDK.hero.description.message: Development entry points for connecting your function capabilities to different delivery channels. Use SDKs to plug local code into Connect, Cloud, MCP, and Fusion services instead of rewriting glue code.

SDK.category.connect.description.message: Expose functions or services that already run locally, so remote API access and MCP access become direct and controlled.

SDK.connect.mcp.description.message: Expose local functions as remotely callable MCP tools so agents use the capability exactly where it already runs.

SDK.connect.api.description.message: Expose local functions or services as remote APIs, useful when you want to start locally and open access step by step.

SDK.category.cloud.description.message: Hand the same function capability over to OOMOL Cloud and reduce the infrastructure work behind service delivery.

SDK.cloud.mcp.description.message: Provide functions deployed on OOMOL Cloud directly as MCP tools for agent use.

SDK.block.mcp.description.message: Wrap function tools or function capabilities as MCP tool interfaces with less boilerplate between implementation and tool delivery.

SDK.cloud.task.python.description.message: Write function tasks in Python that run directly on OOMOL Cloud.

SDK.cloud.task.ts.description.message: Write function tasks in TypeScript that run directly on OOMOL Cloud.

SDK.cta.description.message: View complete API documentation and sample code to quickly get started with OOMOL SDK development

SDK.category.fusion.description.message: Call OOMOL Fusion APIs through one SDK and avoid rebuilding polling and async handling yourself.

SDK.fusion.ts.description.message: Call OOMOL Fusion APIs from TypeScript and plug more capabilities into your functions or services.

SDK.fusion.py.description.message: Call OOMOL Fusion APIs from Python and plug more capabilities into your functions or services.

SDK.fusion.feature3.message: Support text-to-image, TTS and more services

HOME.Downloads.page.description.message: Install oo-cli to connect agents to SaaS tools and published capabilities, then download OOMOL Studio when you need to build and validate custom tools locally.
